findings.message: Target PR #7 changes only static frontend files and documentation for local draft-mode interactivity.
evidence: GitHub changed-file list returned README.md, index.html, package.json, src/data/templates.js, src/main.js, and src/styles.css.

findings.message: The app adds the requested draft-mode interactions without changing source-of-truth semantics.
evidence: Tabs cover Dashboard, Clients, Accounts, Content Calendar, Post Packages, Approval Queue, Weekly Report, Settings, and Blocked Actions.
Post packages expand to show hook, caption, CTA, platform, content pillar, proof note, risk note, owner_approved_draft status, and live-posting block.
Calendar day, platform, and content-pillar filters are browser-local preferences.

findings.message: Mandatory live-action blocks remain present after the interactive UI update.
evidence: No fetch, XMLHttpRequest, tracking, or analytics client code was added.
All accounts remain not_connected and draft_only with credentialsStored false.
Copy and download actions are browser-local and do not send data.

findings.message: Safe target checks passed locally before merge.

optionalImprovements: Add browser-based interaction tests later if the repo adopts a test runner.
Keep official handles pending until the owner provides exact public handles.
Keep OAuth, scheduling, posting, analytics, DMs/comments, and n8n activation as separate approval-gated milestones.
limitations: Critique is advisory only and cannot approve, merge, deploy, connect accounts, schedule, post, use credentials, call analytics APIs, activate automations, or create accepted lessons.
Playwright was unavailable in the target repo, so browser automation was not run.
Review did not alter target repository settings, sensitive values, live social accounts, production deployment, or domain/DNS.
